title: Clubfoot
text: Home * Engines * Clubfoot Clubfoot logo [1] Clubfoot , a very basic UCI open source chess engine by Shawn Chidester , written in C++ . It was started as a real-world example of how to implement a UCI chess engine using the Senjo UCI adapter [2] . It uses 0x88 board representation, null move pruning , PVS with aspiration windows , iterative deepening , IID , transposition table , killer heuristic , check extensions , and late move reductions using a custom version of the history heuristic - among other common concepts. This engine is available as source code [3] , and Dann Corbit has provided binaries [4] . Links to 32 and 64 bit binaries for Windows and Linux are also available in the Clubfoot README, found in the github source repository [5] . Contents 1 Selected Games 2 See also 3 Forum Posts 4 External Links 4.1 Chess Engine 4.2 Misc 5 References Selected Games [ edit ] Here is a sample 5 minute game to demonstrate the strength - or lack thereof -
